cards.front: Which two raw materials are used in the Haber process?
cards.explanation: The Haber process uses nitrogen and hydrogen as its raw materials to manufacture ammonia, which can be used to produce nitrogen-based fertilisers.

choices.feedback: Correct: nitrogen and hydrogen are the raw materials for the Haber process.
Oxygen is not listed; hydrogen is the second raw material.
Ammonia is manufactured by the process; it is not listed as a raw material.

cards.sourceExcerpt:
The Haber process is used to manufacture ammonia, which can be
used to produce nitrogen-based fertilisers.
The raw materials for the Haber process are nitrogen and
hydrogen.
cards.sourceLocator: Official PDF pages 88-89; 4.10.4 The Haber process and the use of NPK fertilisers (chemistry only)

cards.front: Compounds of which three elements do NPK fertilisers contain?
cards.explanation: NPK fertilisers contain compounds of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium, which are used as fertilisers to improve agricultural productivity.

choices.feedback: Correct: NPK fertilisers contain compounds of all three elements.
Calcium replaces potassium, which is one of the three named elements.
Sulfur replaces phosphorus, which is one of the three named elements.

cards.sourceExcerpt:
Compounds of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium are used as
fertilisers to improve agricultural productivity. NPK fertilisers contain
compounds of all three elements.
cards.sourceLocator: Official PDF pages 88-89; 4.10.4.2 Production and uses of NPK fertilisers
